raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/scrub> 2025 I ordered a Vehicle Report from XXXX ( a.k.a. XXXX XXXX XXXX located in XXXX, IL ) for {$49.00} and paid using my Citibank credit card. The report received did not contain any extra information compared to a free report I got from the web. Therefore, on XX/XX/scrub> 2025 I initiated a dispute for the charge siting a low quality of the received product. 
Two days later a representative from XXXX XXXX contacted me by email inquiring about a nature of my dispute, which I explained in my response to the company. The representative followed up right away by saying that they accept my dissatisfaction and will reimburse me, if I withdraw my dispute. After a couple of back and forth emails where I inquired about the company assurance of the refund I withdrew my dispute. After that I never heard back from the company- my emails went unanswered, neither I received any refund. 
i called the credit card, explained the situation that I was basically tricked into withdrawing my dispute and the customer service representative re-opened my dispute. A couple of days later I got a letter from the credit card saying that since I withdrew the dispute myself I have to contact the merchant and resolve the problem directly. The merchant, of course, does not respond any longer. End of story. 
Please, assist. 
Thank you!
